summary: Frontend-only visual/theme test of Fork·Fate light-mode recolor + Favorites theming. Toggle word label works; sage/tan accent recolor works for spin-roulette-button and mode-food. HOWEVER the header Favorites BUTTON and the Favorites DRAWER remain in the DARK palette while in light mode — the fix did NOT take effect at runtime. Zero JS console errors. Dark-mode regression is clean.

ui_bugs.issue: In LIGHT mode computed color is rgb(255,255,255) (white) on the near-white/cream header — button is effectively invisible. Expected the light-palette trigger classes (border-[#E4E4E7] text-[#3F3F46]) to apply but the dark palette (text-white) is still in use.
In LIGHT mode drawer computed background is rgb(14,14,14) and text rgb(255,255,255) — expected cream/light surface (#FBF7EF) with dark text. Screenshot confirms dark drawer on light page.

integration_issues.issue: useTheme() is implemented as isolated local useState in each component (no Context/store/storage-event). When Home.jsx toggles the theme, FavoritesDrawer's own useTheme() state does NOT update. Its `light` boolean stays false, so P stays on the dark palette even after the <html data-ff-theme> attribute flips to 'light'. Root cause of both bugs above.

after_light_toggle.fav_color: rgb(255,255,255) BUG — should be dark grey (~#3F3F46)

action_items: Refactor useTheme.js to a shared source of truth so ALL consumers re-render on toggle. Options: (a) wrap App in a ThemeContext.Provider that owns theme+toggle and exposes via useContext, or (b) add a `window` event ('ff-theme-change') that useTheme instances subscribe to in a useEffect, or (c) listen to `storage` events + a same-tab custom event. Currently only the component that owns the toggle sees state changes; FavoritesDrawer is stuck on its initial 'dark' palette.
After the fix, confirm: light-mode open-favorites-button computed color ≠ rgb(255,255,255) and favorites-drawer computed background ≠ rgb(14,14,14).
critical_code_review_comments: useTheme is a per-component useState — behaves like a private hook, not a shared store. Any component reading `theme` will be desynced from a sibling that calls toggle(). Move to Context or an event-based subscription immediately.

The scoped CSS overrides in index.css (data-ff-theme=light [data-ff-scope=app] ...) do NOT reach FavoritesDrawer content because SheetContent is portaled OUTSIDE the [data-ff-scope='app'] subtree — so even class-based overrides can't rescue it. The JS-side theme check must work for the drawer.

success_rate.frontend: 60% — 4/6 requirements pass; 2 critical light-mode Favorites bugs remain

context_for_next_testing_agent: Skip intro via localStorage 'ff_guided_seen'='1'. Do NOT click Deal/Spin — API quota. After main agent fixes useTheme sharing, re-run the same computed-style checks: open-favorites-button color should be dark on light; favorites-drawer bg should be ~rgb(251,247,239). Iteration 54 previously passed; the regression is that Home.jsx and FavoritesDrawer no longer share theme state after the recent theme-aware rewrite.
rca of the issue: useTheme() in /app/frontend/src/hooks/useTheme.js uses `useState` with a localStorage initializer but exposes no shared store. Home.jsx calls toggle() → updates ONLY Home's copy of state and writes localStorage. FavoritesDrawer's independent useTheme() instance never re-renders because (1) it doesn't listen to storage events, (2) there is no context, (3) there is no pub/sub. So `light` stays false there, and P stays on the dark palette. Fix: introduce ThemeContext OR dispatch a CustomEvent on toggle and subscribe in useEffect.
